Feature Description: It should be understood that for most coverpoints that are based on arithmatic instructions, the coverpoints will check to make sure they're non-trivial, meaning that vl != 0, vstart=0, vill=0, and they do not trap. It may be useful to know that when using vsetvl{i} instructions if rd != x0 and rs1 = x0, vl = vlmax

Goal: Confirm vector floating point instructions set mstatus.FS to dirty when modifying floating point register state
Feature Description: Set mstatus.FS = {initial/clean} and run a fd writing instruction
Spec: Any vector floating-point instruction that modifies any floating-point extension state (i.e., floating-point CSRs or f registers) must set mstatus.FS to Dirty.

Goal: Confirm vector floating point instructions set mstatus.FS to dirty when modifying floating point csr state
Feature Description: Set mstatus.FS = {initial/clean} and run an instruction that would raise a fp flag after clearing the fp flags
Spec: Any vector floating-point instruction that modifies any floating-point extension state (i.e., floating-point CSRs or f registers) must set mstatus.FS to Dirty.

Goal: Confirm vector floating point instructions propperly set mstatus.FS when not modifying floating point state
Feature Description: Set mstatus.FS = {initial/clean} and run a vector fp instruction that does not affect floating point state (chosen situation for coverpoint is a .vv ins where vs1 = vs2 = 0)
Expectation: mstatus.FS may be set to dirty or not depending on implementation
Spec: Any vector floating-point instruction that modifies any floating-point extension state (i.e., floating-point CSRs or f registers) must set mstatus.FS to Dirty.

Goal: Confrim that vector floating point instructions (even if they dont modify fp state) raise an illegal instruction exception if mstatus.FS is set to off
Feature Description: Set mstatus.FS = {off} and run a vector fp instruction that does not affect floating point state  (chosen situation for coverpoint is a .vv ins where vs1 = vs2 = 0)
Spec: If the floating-point unit status field mstatus.FS is Off then any attempt to execute a vector floating-point instruction will raise an illegal instruction exception.
